steps.title: Find the relationship between the two concepts
steps.content:
Sentence 3 describes the cohesion species concept, which defines species by phenotypic cohesion.

Sentence 4 introduces the genetic species concept, which organizes species by genetic similarity.

The passage is presenting two different, competing approaches to the same problem—not one building on the other, but contrasting with it.

steps.title: Match the transition to the contrast
steps.content:
“On the other hand” signals that a second approach or viewpoint is being introduced in contrast to the first.

The cohesion concept and genetic concept use entirely different criteria (phenotypic vs. genetic), so they are alternative frameworks competing within the field—exactly what “on the other hand” conveys.

A, B, and C each misread the relationship: “for instance” treats it as an example of the cohesion concept, “therefore” suggests a consequence, and “in fact” adds emphasis rather than introducing an alternative.

D is correct.
